title: Pseudorlaya maritima
intro: Pseudorlaya maritima, also known as Sea Fennel or Sea Cynara, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Pseudorlaya maritima include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pseudorlaya maritima is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, often used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion, as well as to alleviate respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Pseudorlaya maritima belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a genus of flowering plants that is native to the Mediterranean region. The family Apiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many edible and medicinal species. The order of Pseudorlaya maritima is Apiales, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their umbrella-shaped inflorescences. The kingdom of Pseudorlaya maritima is Plantae, which is the group of plants that are characterized by their cell walls containing cellulose and chloroplasts.
